title: Proofpoint Email Gateway TLS and Transport Posture (MTA Logs)
app: Proofpoint On Demand Email Security add-on or TA-proofpoint (org-specific syslog/HEC parsing)
spl:
index=mail sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval tls=lower(coalesce(smtp_tls_status, TLS_Status, tls_status, ""))
| eval tls_ok=if(match(tls,"none|no|plaintext|not used|off"),0,1)
| where tls_ok=0
| stats count values(rcpt) as sample_rcpt by sender, client_ip
| sort -count
description: Inbound or outbound messages where SMTP TLS was not used exposes messages to passive surveillance and fails many contractual encryption expectations.
value: Extends the same TLS visibility pattern used in HIPAA-style audits to continuous monitoring, letting messaging teams chase down legacy apps still sending cleartext.
implementation: Field names (`smtp_tls_status`, `rcpt`, `client_ip`) depend on your Proofpoint log export and Splunk parser—align with `props.conf` from your TA. Scope to regulated business units via recipient domain lookups. Pair with connector remediation tickets.
visualization: Table (sender IP × recipient samples), map of client_ip, timeline of cleartext events.

references.title: Proofpoint On Demand Email Security Add-on (Splunkbase)

knownFalsePositives: Opportunistic TLS retries, old appliances, and partner MTAs on legacy ciphers in transition.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
